name: Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer Studios Inc. v. Grokster, Ltd.
summary: Held that distributing a device with the object of promoting its use to infringe copyright can create secondary liability, adopting an inducement theory for copyright law.
significance: Cited for its holding that liability cannot be predicated on 'mere failure to take affirmative steps to prevent infringement' and for establishing inducement as a basis for contributory copyright liability.
